div.h2: EPOLLHUP and EPOLLERR: Kernel-Reported Events → REACTOR_ERROR Translation

div.p:
⚠ Invariant: EPOLLHUP and EPOLLERR are delivered by the kernel regardless of subscription.
You cannot opt out. Always check REACTOR_ERROR before REACTOR_READ in every callback.
Order: if (events & REACTOR_ERROR) { close(); return; } then handle READ/WRITE.

text.tspan: // Canonical reactor I/O callback — correct error-check ordering

g.text: void http_io_callback(int fd, uint32_t events, void *udata) {

text.tspan: // ① ERROR check FIRST — cannot be deferred

g.text: conn_close(conn); // timer cancel → deregister → close(fd) → free
return; // NEVER fall through to READ/WRITE after error

text.tspan: // ② READ — safe only if no error

g.text: if (conn->state == CLOSING) return; // read may have closed it

text.tspan: // ③ WRITE — safe only if connection still alive
// Canonical reactor I/O callback — correct error-check ordering
// ① ERROR check FIRST — cannot be deferred
// NEVER fall through to READ/WRITE after error
// ② READ — safe only if no error
// ③ WRITE — safe only if connection still alive

div.p:
Asynchronous socket error
— RST segment received from peer
— Non-blocking connect() failed
— I/O error at network layer
— Out-of-band data condition

ol.li:
getsockopt(fd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_ERROR, &err, &len)
to retrieve the specific errno

Close the connection immediately
Do NOT attempt further reads/writes

div.p:
Peer closed its write side
— TCP FIN received (remote half-close)
— Receive buffer may still have data
— No new data will arrive after drain
— Common on normal connection close

ol.li:
Read any remaining data still in
the kernel receive buffer

On n == 0: close the connection
Connection is half-closed; writes may still work

div.p:
Specific remote half-close signal
— Linux kernel ≥ 2.6.17 only
— More precise than EPOLLHUP
— Peer sent TCP FIN (write-side closed)
— Must explicitly subscribe: add
EPOLLRDHUP to epoll_ctl flags

ol.li: Stop reading: no new data will arrive
Optionally flush any pending write buffer

Begin graceful close sequence
Faster detection than waiting for read() → 0

div.p:
Both flags delivered simultaneously
— Data available in receive buffer AND
— Asynchronous error has also occurred
— Common during RST with queued data
— Also occurs on EPOLLHUP + data present
— Can arrive in a single epoll_wait event
Data after an error is unreliable; discard it
